intro: Kohleria krameriana, also known as Kramer's Kohleria, is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Kohleria krameriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Kohleria krameriana is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
